instruction: Classify the farmer's described crop problem into a cause category.
baseline_prompt: You are a crop-advisory assistant. Classify the farmer's described crop problem into a cause category. Classify into one of these categories: pest, disease, nutrient, water_stress, weather_damage, weed.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: There are swarms of locusts that descended on my sorghum field yesterday. They've stripped the leaves down to the stems in large patches and are still feeding actively.

Citrus trees are oozing sticky brown gum from the trunk near ground level. The bark in that area is darkened and cracked. Leaves are yellowing and dropping on entire branches, and some twigs are dying back from the tips.

The apple trees in my orchard have leaves that are turning yellow between the veins, especially on the younger branches. Fruit set has been poor this year and what fruit there is seems small and underdeveloped.
The rice paddy in the northeast corner shows stunted plants with dark green leaves and reduced tillering. That section has been flooded six inches deeper than optimal for three weeks.
The pepper plants in the eastern section have broken branches and shredded leaves after yesterday's windstorm with gusts measured at 75 kilometers per hour.
My potato field is being overrun by lamb's quarters. These unwanted plants are almost waist-high now and blocking sunlight from reaching my potato plants.
I'm finding tiny puncture marks on my apple fruits, and many have brown tunnels inside when I cut them open. Small white maggots are present in the damaged areas.

My rose bushes have black spots with feathery margins on the leaves. The tissue around the spots turns yellow before the entire leaf falls off. Almost all the lower foliage is gone now and it's progressing upward.

I'm noticing that my wheat crop has a general pale appearance across the entire field. The plants seem weak and the leaf tips are showing some browning. We haven't had any unusual weather and I didn't apply the usual amendments this spring.
My tomato plants have wilting leaves even though it's not too hot, and the soil feels bone dry an inch below the surface. I haven't been able to irrigate for three weeks due to pump issues.
There are brown, papery spots on my apple fruits with small dents and bruises. We had a sudden thunderstorm with heavy ice falling for about fifteen minutes two days ago.
My cotton field has been invaded by morning glory vines that are wrapping around the stems and pulling them down. They're spreading through the entire field.
Rats or mice are destroying my stored grain in the warehouse. I can see gnaw marks on the sacks and there are droppings everywhere mixed with the damaged grain.

I'm seeing white powdery coating on the upper surface of my cucumber leaves. It started on older leaves but now younger ones are getting covered too. The leaves are curling and the plant growth has slowed down significantly.

The corn stalks in my field are stunted and much shorter than they should be at this stage. The lower leaves have developed a purple tinge along the edges and undersides. Growth has been very slow despite adequate rainfall this season.

test.query: My cucumber vines are wilting badly by midday even though I'm watering every evening. The leaves stay limp and some fruits are developing white patches and stopping growth.
Large portions of my cabbage heads are bruised and damaged with visible impact marks. A hailstorm passed through the area yesterday evening with ice stones nearly three centimeters in diameter.
Wild mustard plants have appeared throughout my canola field. They look similar to my crop but are growing more aggressively and I can see them competing for resources.
My bean plants have leaves that are completely skeletonized - only the veins remain. I caught several metallic-colored beetles actively feeding during my morning inspection.

My tomato plants have developed dark brown spots on the lower leaves that are spreading upward. The spots have yellow halos around them and the affected leaves are turning completely yellow and dropping off. I've noticed this started after several days of humid weather.

My citrus trees have new leaves coming in much smaller and with a mottled yellow-green pattern. Fruit production has declined significantly over the past two seasons and what fruit does develop often drops prematurely before ripening.
The corn leaves are rolling inward and turning grayish-green. We've had no significant rainfall in over a month and my irrigation system can only cover half the field.
Strong winds last Tuesday night flattened about 60 percent of my soybean crop. Many plants are now lodged at the base and lying horizontally on the ground.
There are these broad-leafed plants spreading rapidly across my soybean plot. They weren't there last month but now they're everywhere and choking out my seedlings.
I've got aphids covering the new growth on my cabbage crop. The colonies are so dense that the leaves are curling up and the plants have stopped growing properly.

Grapevines are showing downy growth on the underside of leaves, with corresponding yellow patches on top. Young shoots and flower clusters have a grayish fuzzy appearance. Infected berries are shriveling and turning brown.

Cotton plants are showing reddish-purple discoloration on the leaves and stems. Growth has been severely restricted and the plants are much smaller than normal for this time in the season. Root development appears limited when I checked a few plants.
My strawberry plants have brown leaf margins and the fruit is small and dry. The soil moisture meter shows readings well below the recommended range for the past month.
After the cold snap three nights ago, my potato plants have blackened foliage that looks water-soaked and limp. The temperature sensor recorded minus one degree for about four hours before dawn.
There's a lot of crabgrass coming up in my tomato beds. The patches are getting thicker every day and my tomato plants look stunted where the grass is densest.
The ears on my corn have been invaded by worms that are eating the kernels. When I peel back the husks, I find brown frass and damaged grain at the tips.

The apple trees have dark, sunken cankers on branches and the bark is peeling away in those areas. Some fruit shows brown circular spots that are slightly sunken with concentric rings. Leaves near affected branches are dropping prematurely.

My tomato plants have yellowing leaves starting from the bottom, and the older leaves are turning pale while the new growth looks okay. The veins remain green but the tissue between them is becoming lighter. I've been watering regularly and don't see any insects.
My pepper plants have blossom drop and the leaves feel papery to touch. The drip irrigation line was clogged for two weeks before I noticed, and now the plants look really stressed.
My tomato field was hit by a severe storm yesterday afternoon with ice pellets the size of marbles. Now the plants have torn leaves, broken stems, and many fruits have deep pockmarks and bruising.
I'm seeing purple nutsedge taking over sections of my rice paddy. It's growing faster than the rice and forming dense patches that are shading my crop.

My corn stalks have been completely knocked down and partially eaten near the base. I found tracks and droppings that look like they're from wild boar or deer that have been getting into the field at night.

My pepper plants are producing very few flowers and the leaves have developed brown spots that started at the edges and are moving inward. The soil was tested two years ago but I haven't added anything since then. Plants look weak overall.
The beans in the low-lying section of my field have yellow leaves and some root rot symptoms. That area tends to hold standing water, and we've had heavy rains for ten straight days.
